channel.title: Surfaced — Discover What's Next
channel.description: Fresh discoveries, trending products, hidden gems, future tech, and daily tools — curated every day.

item.title: Lola Visual
item.description: Lola Visual is an innovative AI-powered assistant that integrates directly into your design workflow to help generate and refine visual assets. It leverages artificial intelligence to offer suggestions for color palettes, typography, layout variations, and even to generate initial design concepts based on textual prompts. Lola Visual aims to augment the creative process, speeding up exploration and decision-making for designers. It operates on a freemium model, offering core functionalities for free with advanced features available through subscription.

item.title: Ray Studio
item.description: Ray Studio is a powerful, collaborative design tool focused on creating vector graphics and user interface elements. It offers a rich set of features for illustration, prototyping, and design system management, all within a real-time, multi-user environment. Designers can work together seamlessly on the same canvas, iterating rapidly and sharing feedback instantly. Ray Studio is available through a freemium model, with a robust free tier suitable for individual creators and small teams.

item.title: eve
item.description: eve is a cutting-edge framework designed for building sophisticated agents. It provides a structured and scalable environment for developers to create intelligent systems capable of complex tasks and interactions. By abstracting away much of the underlying complexity, eve allows for rapid development and deployment of agent-based applications. The framework is open-source and free to use, fostering a collaborative ecosystem.

item.title: torlink
item.description: torlink is a streamlined, zero-configuration torrent finder and downloader that operates directly within your terminal. It eliminates the need for separate graphical applications or web interfaces to search for and acquire torrent files. Designed for efficiency and speed, it allows users to quickly locate desired content and initiate downloads without ever leaving their command-line environment. This tool is open-source and free to use.

item.title: wloc
item.description: wloc is a sophisticated tool designed to modify your Apple device's network-based location (gs-loc). It seamlessly integrates with popular proxy tools like Surge, Quantumult X, and Loon, allowing for precise control over your device's perceived geographical position. With a user-friendly shortcut system, you can instantly set or restore your actual location, offering unparalleled flexibility for privacy, testing, or accessing geo-restricted content. The tool is completely free to use.

item.title: Appwrite
item.description: Appwrite is an open-source backend platform that gives you authentication, databases, file storage, serverless functions, and realtime APIs out of one self-hostable box (or their managed cloud, with a generous free tier). You get SDKs for web, Flutter, React Native, and most server languages, so the same backend serves every client you ship. It started as a weekend project and grew into one of the fastest-growing developer platforms on GitHub, now past 50,000 stars.

item.title: Ollama
item.description: Ollama is a lightweight, open-source framework for downloading, running, and managing large language models entirely on your own hardware. One terminal command pulls a model like Llama or Mistral and starts chatting with it — no API keys, no cloud account, no per-token bills. It exposes a clean local API that plugs into editors, chat UIs, and scripts, and it handles the messy parts (quantized weights, GPU/CPU juggling, model updates) for you. It runs on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

item.title: Ciphertext
item.description: Ciphertext is a web-based audio editing tool designed for content creators, particularly podcasters, simplifying the process of cleaning up and enhancing audio. It uses AI to automatically remove filler words, pauses, and background noise, allowing users to focus on their content rather than tedious editing tasks. The platform offers a freemium model, with a free tier for basic edits and paid plans for more advanced features and longer audio durations. It's built for speed and ease of use, making professional-sounding audio accessible without extensive technical knowledge.

item.title: Nuclino
item.description: Nuclino is a unified workspace for organizing and sharing your team's knowledge. It allows you to create and connect documents, build a team wiki, manage projects, and keep track of important information all in one place. It uses a visual, interconnected approach to knowledge management, making it easy to find and navigate information. Nuclino offers a free plan for small teams and paid plans for larger organizations, with a focus on real-time collaboration and a clean, intuitive interface.

item.title: Krisp
item.description: Krisp is an AI-powered application that eliminates background noise from your voice calls and online meetings. It works by processing your audio in real-time, removing distracting sounds like keyboard typing, barking dogs, or traffic, ensuring your voice comes through clearly. Krisp is available as a freemium service, with free usage limits and paid subscriptions for unlimited use and additional features. Its primary advantage is its ability to drastically improve audio clarity in any communication scenario, making remote work and online interactions much smoother and more professional.

item.title: TinyMed
item.description: TinyMed is a free, open-source, and privacy-focused application designed for tracking personal health metrics and symptoms in a minimalist, user-friendly interface. It allows individuals to log daily entries related to mood, sleep, diet, medication, and custom symptoms without the overwhelming complexity of many health apps. The app's core value lies in its simplicity and its commitment to keeping all data locally on the user's device, ensuring complete privacy. TinyMed is particularly adept at helping users identify patterns and correlations between lifestyle choices and their physical or mental well-being through straightforward data visualization.

item.title: T3MP3ST
item.description: T3MP3ST is an advanced, autonomous red teaming platform designed for offensive security operations. It utilizes a multi-agent meta-harness architecture to simulate sophisticated cyberattacks, allowing security professionals to test and strengthen their defenses against emergent threats. The platform is built with TypeScript and offers a highly customizable framework for automating complex penetration testing scenarios, identifying vulnerabilities, and assessing an organization's resilience in a dynamic digital landscape. It operates on a free, open-source model.

item.title: Perchance
item.description: Perchance is a free, web-based tool that allows users to create their own random generators. It uses a simple, intuitive grammar to define lists of words, phrases, or even entire sentences, which can then be combined in various ways to produce unique outputs. This is incredibly useful for creative endeavors such as writing fiction, generating game content, brainstorming ideas, or even creating unique names. Perchance is particularly powerful for its ease of use; you don't need to be a programmer to create sophisticated generators, and the results can be shared with others via a simple link.

item.title: Zettlr
item.description: Zettlr is a free and open-source Markdown editor specifically designed for academics and researchers. It facilitates the creation and management of research notes, bibliographies, and full manuscripts using Markdown. The tool integrates with Zotero for seamless citation management and supports exporting to various formats like PDF and Word. Its core value lies in its ability to help users organize complex research projects and write papers efficiently, with a particular strength in managing citations and references directly within the writing environment.

item.title: Linguee
item.description: Linguee is a powerful multilingual dictionary and search engine that provides contextualized translations of words and phrases. Unlike traditional dictionaries that offer single definitions, Linguee searches a vast database of translated texts from official sources and multilingual websites, showing you how terms are actually used in different languages in real-world contexts. This allows for more nuanced and accurate translations, especially for technical or idiomatic expressions. It supports a wide range of language pairs and is freely accessible online and through its mobile app.

item.title: Hypothes.is
item.description: Hypothes.is is a free, open-source platform that enables users to annotate and discuss any web page or PDF. It functions as a collaborative annotation layer on top of the internet, allowing individuals and groups to highlight text, add notes, and share insights publicly or privately. This tool is particularly useful for scholars, students, and anyone engaged in critical reading and research, turning static online content into interactive discussions. It allows for layered conversations, making it possible to build upon existing annotations or create entirely new threads of thought around a piece of content.

item.title: Internet Archive's Wayback Machine
item.description: The Wayback Machine, operated by the Internet Archive, is a digital archive of the World Wide Web that allows users to revisit and explore past versions of websites. By crawling and archiving billions of web pages over time, it provides a unique historical record of the internet. Users can input a URL and see snapshots of that website from different dates, offering insights into how content has evolved, disappeared, or been preserved. This service is entirely free to use and is invaluable for historical research, recovering lost content, or understanding the development of online information.

item.title: Kumu.io
item.description: Kumu.io is a powerful and intuitive platform for visualizing complex systems, stakeholder networks, and feedback loops. It allows users to map out interconnected elements, understand relationships, and identify leverage points within a system. The tool offers both free and paid tiers, with generous features for individual use and robust options for teams and organizations. Kumu excels at making complex interconnectedness tangible and understandable, turning abstract ideas into clear, explorable visual maps.

item.title: Gather Town
item.description: Gather Town is a 2D virtual space where you can create interactive environments for socializing, meetings, and events. Users navigate these spaces as customizable avatars, allowing for spontaneous conversations and a sense of presence often missing in traditional video calls. It operates on a freemium model, with a robust free tier for individuals and small teams, and paid tiers offering more features and capacity. It excels at fostering casual interactions and community building in a digital format, mimicking the serendipity of in-person gatherings.

item.title: Fable
item.description: Fable is a mobile-first role-playing game designed for social storytelling and collaborative adventure. It allows groups of friends to embark on shared quests, creating and developing characters together in a persistent digital world. The game emphasizes player-driven narratives and cooperative gameplay, where decisions made by one player can impact the experiences of others. Fable is free-to-play, with optional in-app purchases for cosmetic items and convenience features. Its unique value lies in its ability to facilitate rich, emergent storytelling through a shared gaming experience, acting as a digital tabletop for friends to craft epic tales.

item.title: Mastodon
item.description: Mastodon is a free, open-source, decentralized social networking platform that operates on a network of independent servers, known as instances. Users can join existing instances or even host their own. It functions similarly to other microblogging platforms, allowing users to post short messages (toots), follow others, and engage in discussions. The key difference is its decentralized nature, which means no single entity controls the platform, fostering a more community-driven and ad-free experience. Mastodon prioritizes user privacy and control over their data and feed, avoiding algorithmic manipulation.

item.title: ElevenLabs
item.description: ElevenLabs is a powerful AI-powered platform that excels at generating incredibly realistic and emotionally nuanced synthetic voices from text. It offers a wide range of pre-made AI voices and allows users to clone their own voice or create entirely new custom voices with just a few minutes of audio input. The platform is freemium, with a generous free tier for experimentation and paid plans for professional use, offering features like longer audio generation and higher quality output. ElevenLabs stands out by its unparalleled ability to capture subtle intonations and emotional delivery, making AI-generated speech indistinguishable from human recordings for many applications.

item.title: Context.dev
item.description: Context.dev is a powerful API designed to help AI products and developers scrape, enrich, and extract data from the internet. It provides tools to capture content from any URL, crawl entire websites, and convert web pages into LLM-ready Markdown. Beyond basic scraping, it offers features like structured data extraction into custom schemas and screenshot capture. This service is particularly vital for building AI applications that require real-time or comprehensive web data. Context.dev operates on a paid, usage-based model, offering a scalable solution for developers needing robust web data capabilities.

item.title: Acti
item.description: Acti is an innovative agentic keyboard for mobile devices that transforms how you interact with your smartphone. Instead of switching between apps for information or actions, Acti understands your intent expressed through natural language typed into its special bar. Whether you need live sports scores, restaurant recommendations, to add an event to your calendar, or to find information within your connected apps like Notion, Acti brings the relevant result or action directly to you, in context. It aims to streamline mobile tasks, reducing the need for extensive app navigation. Acti is currently free to try, with potential for future premium features.

item.title: oomwoo
item.description: oomwoo is an ambitious open-source project aiming to create a fully functional, DIY vacuum robot cleaner. It leverages Python for its core logic, allowing enthusiasts and developers to customize and improve its navigation, cleaning patterns, and sensor integration. The project is ideal for those interested in robotics, home automation, and learning about the inner workings of cleaning robots. It's a free, community-driven initiative, offering a transparent and adaptable alternative to proprietary robotic vacuums.

item.title: KanbanFlow
item.description: KanbanFlow is a free, web-based Kanban board designed to help individuals and teams visualize their workflow and manage tasks efficiently. It supports a highly customizable board setup with swimlanes, task columns, and the ability to assign tasks, set deadlines, and track progress. A key feature is the integrated Pomodoro timer, which encourages focused work intervals. It's ideal for anyone looking to streamline project management and improve personal or team productivity by breaking down work into manageable stages.

item.title: Tencent EdgeOne Makers
item.description: Tencent EdgeOne Makers is an innovative edge platform that empowers developers to deploy modern web applications and sophisticated AI agents with remarkable speed and ease. It supports popular development frameworks and integrates seamlessly with familiar tools like CLI, Git, and CI/CD pipelines, streamlining the deployment process. The platform offers built-in agent runtime environments, allowing for efficient execution of AI models at the edge. This solution is ideal for developers looking to ship complex applications and AI-driven services rapidly. Pricing is not explicitly detailed but implied to be a service offering.

item.title: openwiki
item.description: OpenWiki is a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed to automate the process of writing and maintaining documentation for your AI agents. It scans your codebase, understands the agent's functions and parameters, and generates clear, structured documentation. This process eliminates the manual, often tedious task of documenting AI systems, ensuring that your agents remain understandable and maintainable as they evolve. OpenWiki is free and open-source, making it an accessible solution for developers working with AI agents.

item.title: Elgato Stream Deck
item.description: The Elgato Stream Deck is a physical device featuring a grid of LCD keys that can be programmed to trigger a vast array of actions with a single press. It's designed to streamline workflows by acting as a dynamic control panel for software, applications, and system functions. Users can assign custom icons to each key, creating visual shortcuts for everything from launching applications and opening specific files to controlling smart home devices and executing complex multi-step macros. The accompanying software is free, and the hardware comes in various sizes at different price points.

item.title: Exploitarium
item.description: Exploitarium is a public repository of proof-of-concept (PoC) exploits and vulnerability research write-ups. This Python-based project aims to consolidate and share publicly available exploit code and detailed analyses of security vulnerabilities. It operates on a free and open-source model, encouraging researchers to report vulnerabilities before they are widely known, potentially allowing them to claim CVEs. The archive's strength lies in its curated collection of actionable exploit code for educational and defensive security purposes.

item.title: Astrid OS Handbook
item.description: The Astrid OS Handbook is an essential guide for anyone looking to contribute to the development of the Astrid operating system. It outlines the project's polyrepo structure, the guiding 'kernel-is-dumb' law, the RFC trigger for proposals, contribution tiers, and the detailed release process. This free, open-source resource is crucial for maintaining a streamlined and collaborative development environment. The handbook is particularly adept at clarifying the nuances of proposing and integrating new features within the Astrid ecosystem.

item.title: Astrid OS Book
item.description: This comprehensive documentation, presented as a book, serves as the definitive guide to the Astrid Operating System. It meticulously details the kernel, capsule management, host ABI, inter-process communication via the bus, and the overall security model. It's a free, open-source resource designed to provide in-depth technical understanding for developers and contributors interested in the inner workings of Astrid OS. The book excels at explaining complex architectural decisions and their implications for system stability and security.

item.title: loop-engineering
item.description: Loop Engineering provides practical patterns, starters, and CLI tools for designing systems that effectively prompt and orchestrate AI coding agents. Inspired by prominent figures in the AI and software design fields, it focuses on building robust and scalable AI agent systems. The project includes utilities like `loop-audit` to help analyze and improve agent behavior, encouraging a systematic approach to developing AI-powered solutions. It's designed for developers who want to create more intelligent and autonomous systems by leveraging the power of multiple AI agents working in concert.

item.title: DeepSpec
item.description: DeepSpec is a full-stack codebase designed for training and evaluating speculative decoding algorithms. These algorithms are crucial for improving the efficiency and speed of large language models (LLMs) by allowing them to predict multiple future tokens in parallel, rather than sequentially. The project provides the necessary tools and infrastructure for researchers and developers to experiment with, implement, and benchmark these advanced decoding strategies, aiming to make LLMs more performant and accessible.

item.title: Goldfish
item.description: Goldfish is an AI-powered writing assistant for Mac that proactively understands your work context. Unlike many AI tools that require extensive prompting and background explanation, Goldfish privately remembers your ongoing projects and writing activities across your Mac. It then uses this context to provide relevant suggestions and improvements directly within any application you're using. This allows for a more seamless and intuitive writing experience, as the AI already knows what you're trying to achieve.

item.title: omnigent
item.description: Omnigent is a powerful open-source AI agent framework and meta-harness designed to orchestrate a diverse range of AI models and custom agents. It allows users to seamlessly swap between different AI backends, including popular options like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Pi, without extensive code rewrites. A key feature is its ability to enforce policies and sandboxing, ensuring secure and controlled execution of AI tasks. This framework simplifies the complex process of integrating and managing multiple AI agents, making it easier to build sophisticated AI-powered applications.

item.title: MiMo-Code
item.description: MiMo-Code is an innovative TypeScript project focused on the co-evolution of AI models and agents. It provides a framework for exploring how these entities can learn from and influence each other over time, pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence development. The project is open-source, encouraging community contributions and experimentation in areas like adaptive learning and multi-agent systems. It aims to facilitate the creation of more sophisticated and autonomous AI systems that can adapt to new information and challenges.

item.title: improve
item.description: improve is an open-source tool designed to leverage your most capable AI models to audit your codebase. It then generates detailed plans for less capable, more cost-effective models to execute. This sophisticated approach allows for high-level code analysis and strategy development using powerful AI, followed by efficient, budget-friendly implementation. It's a smart way to manage AI resources, ensuring that complex tasks are handled by the best tools available, while more routine tasks are optimized for cost and speed. The output is structured plans that guide the execution process.

item.title: Upstream
item.description: Upstream redefines the email experience by creating an inbox designed for seamless collaboration between humans and AI agents. It's built to be a delightful and efficient tool that handles the drudgery of email management. Agents within Upstream can sort incoming messages, draft replies, and proactively manage your communication, all while operating within a beautifully crafted interface. The goal is to make managing your inbox feel light, fast, and even enjoyable, freeing up your mental energy for more important tasks. It promises a significant upgrade from traditional email clients.

item.title: Fundraisly
item.description: Fundraisly is an advanced AI agent specifically designed to revolutionize the fundraising process for startups and businesses. It leverages vast datasets of over 300,000 investors and millions of deals to meticulously identify relevant investors actively funding companies in your specific niche. More importantly, it maps out warm introduction pathways from your existing network to these potential investors, significantly increasing the chances of securing meetings. This intelligent agent automates much of the tedious research and outreach involved in fundraising, allowing entrepreneurs to focus on building their business.

item.title: Homegames
item.description: Homegames.io is an open-source platform designed for the creation and play of games, boasting an eight-year development history. It offers a flexible and robust environment for game developers to build unique game experiences. The platform's architecture encourages community involvement and adherence to open standards, positioning it as a compelling choice for independent developers and enthusiasts seeking an alternative to traditional, proprietary game engines. Its focus on extensibility allows creators to shape their game development workflows and share their creations within a collaborative ecosystem.

item.title: Unlimited-OCR
item.description: Unlimited-OCR is a powerful Python-based open-source project that heralds a new era of 'one-shot long-horizon parsing' using AI. It enables highly efficient and accurate optical character recognition (OCR) capable of processing extensive documents or images in a single pass. This goes beyond typical OCR by offering sophisticated parsing capabilities, allowing for the extraction of complex data structures and contextual information from large volumes of text. It's designed to handle diverse document types and layouts with remarkable precision, making it a significant advancement in document intelligence.

item.title: Yamanote.fun
item.description: Yamanote.fun is a unique web application that recreates the ambient soundscape of Tokyo's Yamanote line, a famously busy and iconic train loop. It allows users to experience the distinct station announcements, train sounds, and general commuter atmosphere, providing an immersive auditory journey. You can virtually 'ride' the line, experiencing the unique sonic identity of each station. This tool is a delightful creation for those interested in urban soundscapes, Japanese culture, or simply seeking a novel and relaxing auditory experience.

item.title: ponytail
item.description: ponytail is a fascinating JavaScript project that aims to make your AI agents think like the laziest, yet most senior, developer in the room. The core philosophy is that the best code is the code that never has to be written. It encourages developers to leverage AI for tasks like refactoring, optimization, and even generating tests, but with a focus on minimizing the actual code produced. This approach promotes elegant solutions and efficient development cycles. The project is open-source and built on a strong community foundation.

item.title: Davit
item.description: Davit is a user interface tool designed specifically for managing Apple's container technologies, such as Docker for Mac. It provides a visual and intuitive way to interact with containers, images, volumes, and networks, offering a more streamlined experience than command-line interfaces. Davit aims to simplify the development workflow for macOS users working with containerized applications, making it easier to build, run, and debug projects that rely on these technologies.

item.title: Clipdrop
item.description: Clipdrop is a suite of AI-powered tools designed to enhance and manipulate images with remarkable ease. It offers features like background removal, object removal, upscaling, relighting, and even generating images from text prompts. These tools leverage advanced artificial intelligence to perform complex editing tasks in seconds, making professional-level image manipulation accessible to everyone. Clipdrop offers a freemium model, with a limited number of free uses for each tool and subscription tiers for unlimited access and higher resolutions. It's exceptionally good at quickly achieving polished visual results without technical expertise.

item.title: Colibri
item.description: Colibri is an open-source project that allows users to run the GLM 5.2 large language model on their own, often less powerful, computers. It focuses on efficient implementation and optimization to make advanced AI accessible even without high-end hardware. The project provides the necessary code and instructions to download, configure, and operate the model locally, offering an alternative to cloud-based AI services. This enables users to experiment with LLMs privately and without continuous internet dependency.

item.title: Revolt
item.description: Revolt is a free and open-source chat platform that prioritizes user privacy and control, offering an alternative to mainstream messaging services. It allows users to create servers, join communities, and communicate through text and voice channels, much like other popular platforms. Revolt's core value lies in its decentralized architecture and commitment to data ownership, ensuring your conversations remain yours. It's entirely free to use, with development funded by donations and community contributions.

item.title: 18 Words
item.description: 18 Words is a simple yet surprisingly effective tool designed to generate exactly 18 words on any given topic. It's built on the idea that concise, constrained expression can lead to more focused and impactful communication. You input a prompt, and the tool provides a curated list of 18 words that aim to capture the essence of your request. It's useful for brainstorming taglines, crafting short descriptions, or even as a creative writing prompt when you're feeling stuck.

item.title: Syncthing
item.description: Syncthing is a free and open-source continuous file synchronization program. It synchronizes files between two or more computers in real time, safely and privately. Unlike cloud-based services, Syncthing is decentralized; there is no central server, and you control your data at all times. It's ideal for synchronizing large volumes of files, including entire project folders or digital asset libraries, across multiple devices without relying on third-party cloud storage. The tool is highly configurable and excellent for ensuring that your important data is consistently backed up and accessible across your personal devices.
